commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The branch updates Codex dynamic-tool projection to quarantine unreadable entries, unreadable fields, and unsupported input schemas before app-server registration, with focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +98, Tests +78. Total +176 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: a dynamic tool named `lookup.ticket` or a duplicate tool name would pass this PR's OpenClaw projection but fail Codex's `validate_dynamic_tools` during `thread/start`. I did not run a live assistant-turn repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can extend the changed projection/quarantine function and tests to cover Codex's documented name, namespace, and duplicate-name rejection cases.

target.title: fix(codex): quarantine unreadable dynamic tools
target.body:
## Summary

- Quarantines Codex dynamic tools with unreadable array entries, unreadable `parameters`, unreadable `description`, or unsupported input schemas before Codex registration.
- Preserves healthy sibling tools so one malformed plugin-owned dynamic tool cannot poison the whole assistant turn before content.
- Emits warning and diagnostic telemetry for quarantined tools while denying calls to quarantined tools in-band.

## Landing note

This PR is intentionally pruned to the runtime quarantine only and is stacked on #88880. #88880 owns provider-safe nullable tool schema projection and prompt snapshot shape; this PR owns only Codex unreadable/unsupported dynamic-tool quarantine. After #88880 lands, retarget this PR to `main`, rebase, and recheck.

## Verification

- Latest head: `fb95836ddba`, restacked on #88880 head `a23ee0b7012`.
- GitHub CI run `26735325010` on `fb95836ddba` passed: 114 jobs succeeded, 7 skipped, 0 failed/cancelled in that run.
- PR rollup after completion: 127 passed, 25 skipped, 0 pending, 0 failing.
- Previous GitHub CI run `26734372195` on `dfe817d364a` passed: 114 jobs succeeded, 7 skipped, 0 failed/cancelled in that run.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts --reporter=dot` passed 1 shard / 39 tests.
- `node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check` passed, `Prompt snapshots are current (7 files).`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts` passed.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- final diff private reported-name scrub passed.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base cron-schema-main-repro-20260601 --prompt ...` reported clean: no accepted/actionable findings, `overall: patch is correct (0.82)`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: one malformed plugin-owned dynamic tool no longer prevents Codex from registering healthy dynamic tools and starting the assistant turn.

Real environment tested: linked OpenClaw gwt worktree stacked on #88880, final head `fb95836ddba`; GitHub CI run `26735325010` passed on that head.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: rebased the pruned quarantine slice onto #88880 head `a23ee0b7012`, reran focused Codex dynamic-tool tests, prompt snapshot check, formatter, lint, diff check, private-name scrub, autoreview, pushed the restacked PR branch, and watched GitHub CI complete.

Evidence after fix: focused regressions pass for unsupported schemas, unreadable tool array entries, unreadable `parameters`, and unreadable `description` while preserving the healthy `message` sibling; autoreview found no actionable defects; the PR compare is back to 2 files, `+201/-25`; GitHub CI run `26735325010` passed.

Observed result after fix: unreadable or unsupported dynamic tools are omitted from Codex specs, recorded in telemetry, logged/diagnosed as quarantined, and their calls return an in-band unknown/unavailable-tool response instead of crashing registration.

What was not tested: live Telegram or full assistant-turn E2E. GitHub CI run `26735325010` passed on `fb95836ddba`.

## Scope note

This PR intentionally does not add new dynamic tool-name policy. Invalid names, reserved MCP prefixes, and length/character restrictions should be handled only if Codex exposes a hard contract that requires them.
target.author: vincentkoc

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
